column3: The creation event for a document. Records the author, the timespan of creation, and the initial text content. May be influenced by existing documents.
Document Note: Document Creation Model Overview

column1: Document Note: Document Creation Model Overview
column3: This model captures the provenance of document creation. The E65 creation event records the author and timespan, and creates the document entity and optionally its initial text content. An optional influence link allows source or reference documents to be recorded. The model is intentionally source-agnostic; wiki page creation, CMS authoring, and manual attribution all map to the same structure. Where this creation represents a new version of an existing document, a link to the previous version can be recorded directly on the created document.
The document that was created. Links back to the simple_document model.

An optional existing document that influenced the creation of this document in a general sense, such as a reference, source, or related work. The influence relationship is intentionally broad and does not imply direct derivation or versioning.

An optional link to the previous version of this document, where this creation event represents a new version of an existing document. The new document should carry an updated version number via P43_has_dimension. The previous version remains accessible via the P130_shows_features_of link in the simple_document model.
The person or organisation who authored this document.
E41: Unique System Label or ID

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID
column3: Required within some documentation or database systems, such as ResearchSpace required label.
The timespan of the creation activity, from the earliest to latest recorded action associated with this creation event.

The initial text content of the document at the point of creation. Preserving this separately ensures the original content remains recoverable even after subsequent updates via the document_update model.
